examples.title: K-Means Clustering Algorithm
examples.description: This pseudocode implements the K-Means clustering algorithm, including the initialization of centroids, the assignment of data points to clusters, and the update of centroids.
examples.code:
INPUT: Data points, Number of clusters (K)
OUTPUT: Cluster assignments

1. Initialize centroids randomly
2. Assign each data point to the cluster with the closest centroid
3. Update centroids as the mean of all data points in each cluster
4. Repeat steps 2-3 until convergence
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implements the K-Means clustering algorithm, which is a type of unsupervised machine learning algorithm used to group similar data points into clusters based on their features.

examples.title: K-Means Clustering Implementation
examples.description: This Python code implements the K-Means clustering algorithm using the scikit-learn library.
examples.code:
from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.datasets import make_blobs

# Generate sample data
X, y = make_blobs(n_samples=100, centers=3, n_features=2, random_state=1)

# Create a KMeans model with 3 clusters
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=3)

# Fit the model to the data
kmeans.fit(X)

# Predict the cluster assignments
y_pred = kmeans.predict(X)
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the K-Means clustering algorithm using the scikit-learn library, which provides a simple and efficient way to perform K-Means clustering.
